rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF LAURREE KELLY RUSSELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/20/21. (No earlier availability.) Attachment 8 to the petition is not correct; the assigned Examiner is the same Examiner who reviewed the petition filed by the now deceased administrator with will annexed, who supplemented his petition three times in order to provide the correct information regarding the decedent's parents. Item 9 of this petition is incomplete. It is unclear how Kathy Duncan, the wife of a post-deceased son of the post-deceased beneficiary, Violet Mae Trammell, has standing. Did Violet Mae Trammell die intestate and was there no estate opened for her? Did William Duncan also die without a will or trust? The other beneficiary, Irene Woods, also post-deceased, but the petition is also silent regarding who takes in her place. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/8/21 to avoid a continuance.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SHELDON A. DULBERG 2010 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/04/2021. 1) The name of the former conservator alleged in paragraph 2 of the amended petition appears incorrect. 2) The date of the former conservator's temporary appointment alleged in paragraph 2 appears incorrect. 3) Please provide proof that the petitioner's professional fiduciary license is current. 4) Paragraph 4c alleges a different amount of compensation awarded to the petitioner as conservator by the order approving the second account from the amount stated in the order. 5) Paragraph 4e alleges that the petitioner has kept all cash in interest-bearing accounts, but Schedule A does not show any interest after 10/18/2018. 6) Please provide the petitioner's time sheets for this account period. 7) Is the petitioner requesting authority to pay herself fees on account between the due date of this account and 12/31/2020? 8) Paragraph 9 alleges that the attorney's hourly rate increased in January 2020, but Exhibit A shows the attorney applied the new rate on 12/06/2019. 8) The bond reduction amount alleged in paragraph 11 appears incorrect. 9) If the petitioner requests the independent power to sell the rental real property, the bond must be increased. The request to reduce the bond appears inconsistent with the request for the independent power. 10) The beginning cash and non-cash carry values do not match the ending cash/non-cash allocation stated in the prior account. 11) Receipts and disbursements must be allocated between principal and income, as the trust is required to distribute income to the beneficiary. CRC 7.901(c).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AJAY KODURI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Administrator filed a "status report" on 12/4/20, not a report of status with a petition for additional time, and without paying a filing fee for the petition to be set for hearing, for which notice would be given, as required by Probate Code Section 12201. As a result of the renewed stay-at-home order issued by the mayor (effective 12/6/20) due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY MAZZA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/20/21. The second amendment filed on 11/2/20 amends the prayer for approval of the executor's "First Report Upon Waiver of Accounting," but the other co-trustees have not waived an accounting. The account is not complete, as the schedule of Receipts is not detailed, as required by Probate Code Section 1062(a). Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Neither the petition nor any amendment addresses the $445 costs (filing fees) shown on Exhibit B to the petition. Is someone seeking to be reimbursed that amount? If there is $259,278.14 in cash on hand as of 5/14/20, after deducting the $8,404.07 statutory fee and the $445 costs, there should be $250,529.07 in cash remaining, not $250,532.07. A third amendment must be filed to address these issues. Courtesy copies of the third amendment and proof of service of the third amendment must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/8/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CRAIG KNUDSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/20/21. (No earlier availability.) Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary was previously confined in any prison or other correctional facility. Receipts for any prior distributions must be filed before final distribution. [LRSF 14.67 L.7.c.] The attorney costs total does not appear to be correct. Additionally, it is not clear why a change of address was required, as none appears to have been filed. The proposed distribution must show the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) and the exact number of shares of each security distributable to each heir, not just 50%. The administrator reports that certain dividends were reinvested, but the report shows fewer shares of Poplar Forest Partners Fund and Lazard US Equity Concentrated than inventoried. The proposed distribution also shows 50% interest to each heir of the Aprilia motorcycle or its sale proceeds if sold "prior to entry of the Court's order," which this Court does not permit. If actual distribution differs from an order for final distribution, an accounting will be required of the transactions that occurred after that order. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/8/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOK CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/20/21. The amended petition is missing the remainder beneficiaries, the San Francisco Exploratorium and the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art. They also did not receive notice. (Attachment 8 must list all persons mentioned in the will as well as all heirs.) The proof of service filed on 10/13/20 was incomplete at item 4. The pleading form of proof of service filed on 10/14/20 is inappropriate; at the very least, if a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice that was served must be attached to it. The proof of service filed on 10/16/20 (no courtesy copy of this one was provided to the Examiner) shows an incomplete Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (items 1, 2, and 3) and item 4 of the proof of service shows that notice was mailed on 10/13/20 before the amended petition was filed on 10/14/20. [LRSF 14.21 A.] The proof of service filed on 10/26/20 is of the incorrect form of notice. A supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner, must be filed to add the remainder beneficiaries to Attachment 8. Proof of service of the Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate for the hearing on 1/20/21 must be served on all interested parties at least 15 days before 1/20/21. Courtesy copies of the supplement and the proof of service must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/8/21.

[CRC 7.902.] A single-sided courtesy copy of the petition and courtesy copies of any other papers (e.g., a supplement to the petition, setting forth the beneficiaries) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/8/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN BENJAMIN STECZYNSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/20/21. Item 8 of the amended petition is missing the alternate executor and contingent beneficiary Myra Jurheé Anneliese (Steczynski) Zerr and the ten charitable beneficiaries. The Notice of Petition to Administer Estate filed on 11/20/20 was incomplete at item 1 and did not clearly indicate that the petitioner seeks to admit a lost will. A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. The will mentions a written statement regarding disposition of tangible personal property, but the petition does not address whether such written statement was found. The declaration rebutting the presumption of revocation would be more persuasive if made by the attorney who reportedly told the petitioner that this lost will was the only one he created for the decedent. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and a new proof of service of the complete Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate, showing that a lost will has been submitted, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/8/21.
